summary: The test suite covers all major functional requirements well: hashing, directory indexing, tree comparison (identical/modified/source-only/target-only), plan generation for all three action types, and both dry-run and execute modes end-to-end. Fixtures use BeforeEach/AfterEach with proper cleanup, tests are well-isolated using random temp directories, and the Describe/Context/It hierarchy is logical and readable. The main weaknesses are in rigor: there is no test for the 'Failed' status path in execute mode (e.g., source file missing at execution time, permission error), no validation that dry-run reports carry Status='WouldExecute', no test for Invoke-SyncPlan with an empty plan, no empty-file hash test, and no coverage of the unknown-action default branch. The forward-slash path in the nested file existence check ('subdir/nested.txt') is also a portability concern on Windows. These gaps mean some error-handling code paths are untested, which limits regression confidence.
